text: DAG-first doesn't enable multi-agent behavior without parallel execution — just a fancier sequential queue
Worker identity/leases must come before DAG — stale recovery assumes single-instance
Security model is routing labels, not enforcement — self-declared submitter, env inheritance, exfiltration via sub-task prompts
Missing dependency failure policy — what happens when a DAG node fails?
Munin coordination uses tag scans and whole-entry rereads — not efficient for dense graphs
General DAG invites cycles/debugging problems — parent/child joins are simpler and sufficient
One scheduler, many workers should be an explicit design invariant
Event-driven promotion needs a reconciliation loop — crash between completion and promotion leaves children blocked
HMAC submitter auth is weak if signing secret is in task's env — doesn't create real privilege boundary
failure_policy at task level is too coarse — should be per-dependency-edge for mixed critical/optional inputs
max_children is only meaningful if enforced by scheduler, not just declared in task metadata
Worker boundary underspecified — is a worker a host daemon, execution slot, or endpoint label?
